paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ye are the children of Jehovah your God: ye shall not cut yourselves, nor make any baldness between your eyes for the dead.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: For thou art a holy people unto Jehovah thy God, and Jehovah hath chosen thee to be a people for his own possession, above all peoples that are upon the face of the earth.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt not eat any abominable thing.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: These are the beasts which ye may eat: the ox, the sheep, and the goat,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: the hart, and the gazelle, and the roebuck, and the wild goat, and the pygarg, and the antelope, and the chamois.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And every beast that parteth the hoof, and hath the hoof cloven in two, [and] cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that may ye eat.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Nevertheless these ye shall not eat of them that chew the cud, or of them that have the hoof cloven: the camel, and the hare, and the coney; because they chew the cud but part not the hoof, they are unclean unto you.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the swine, because he parteth the hoof but cheweth not the cud, he is unclean unto you: of their flesh ye shall not eat, and their carcasses ye shall not touch.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: These ye may eat of all that are in the waters: whatsoever hath fins and scales may ye eat;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and whatsoever hath not fins and scales ye shall not eat; it is unclean unto you.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of all clean birds ye may eat.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But these are they of which ye shall not eat: the eagle, and the gier-eagle, and the ospray,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the glede, and the falcon, and the kite after its kind,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and every raven after its kind,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the ostrich, and the night-hawk, and the sea-mew, and the hawk after its kind,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: the little owl, and the great owl, and the horned owl,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the pelican, and the vulture, and the cormorant,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the stork, and the heron after its kind, and the hoopoe, and the bat.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And all winged creeping things are unclean unto you: they shall not be eaten.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of all clean birds ye may eat.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ye shall not eat of anything that dieth of itself: thou mayest give it unto the sojourner that is within thy gates, that he may eat it; or thou mayest sell it unto a foreigner: for thou art a holy people unto Jehovah thy God. Thou shalt not boil a kid in its mother`s milk.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Thou shalt surely tithe all the increase of thy seed, that which cometh forth from the field year by year.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And thou shalt eat before Jehovah thy God, in the place which he shall choose, to cause his name to dwell there, the tithe of thy grain, of thy new wine, and of thine oil, and the firstlings of thy herd and of thy flock; that thou mayest learn to fear Jehovah thy God always.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And if the way be too long for thee, so that thou art not able to carry it, because the place is too far from thee, which Jehovah thy God shall choose, to set his name there, when Jehovah thy God shall bless thee;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: then shalt thou turn it into money, and bind up the money in thy hand, and shalt go unto the place which Jehovah thy God shall choose:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and thou shalt bestow the money for whatsoever thy soul desireth, for oxen, or for sheep, or for wine, or for strong drink, or for whatsoever thy soul asketh of thee; and thou shalt eat there before Jehovah thy God, and thou shalt rejoice, thou and thy household.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And the Levite that is within thy gates, thou shalt not forsake him; for he hath no portion nor inheritance with thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: At the end of every three years thou shalt bring forth all the tithe of thine increase in the same year, and shalt lay it up within thy gates:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and the Levite, because he hath no portion nor inheritance with thee, and the sojourner, and the fatherless, and the widow, that are within thy gates, shall come, and shall eat and be satisfied; that Jehovah thy God may bless thee in all the work of thy hand which thou doest.
